note: 라이브 mod-ui 호스트에서 덤프. 페달보드 에디터 목업용 이펙터/포트 카탈로그.

plugins.name: AIDA-X
plugins.label: AIDA-X
plugins.comment:
AIDA-X is an Amp Model Player, allowing it to load models of AI trained music gear, which you can then play through!

Its main intended use is to provide high fidelity simulations of amplifiers.
However, it is also possible to run entire signal chains consisting of any combination of amp, cab, dist, drive, fuzz, boost and eq.

plugins.name: amsynth
plugins.label: amsynth
plugins.comment:
The AmSynth is a synth with a classic subtractive synthesizer topology. It features Dual oscillators, a resonant filter, a dual ADSR and a LFO. If you’re not into turning a bunch of fancy knobs and just want to plug and play, give the presets of this synth a go, it has a broad variety of sounds including some classic synths and some funky noises!

Features:
Modeled by Nick Dowell

plugins.name: ALO
plugins.label: ALO
plugins.comment:
ALO is a multi-track looper designed for live audio looping. It works in sync mode, with Global BPM, or in free-running mode.

There are six loops. Press a loop button to:
- arm the loop for recording
- stop playing the loop
- resume the loop

[THRESHOLD] sets the input level in dB that will trigger loop recording.

[BARS] sets the loop length in sync mode (when Global BPM is running). In free running mode, loop length is set at the end of recording the first loop, by activating a different loop button.

[MIDI Base] optionally allows loops to be controlled from a connected MIDI device sending MIDI note on/off messages ([MIDI Base]..[MIDI Base + 5]).

[INSTANT LOOPS] changes the behaviour so some or all loops will stop and resume instantly:
- 0 sets all loops to play from start to finish
- 3 sets loops 1,2 and 3 to play and stop when their loop buttons are pressed
- 6 sets all loops to play and stop when loop buttons are pressed

[CLICK] sets the volume of the click in sync mode, when no loop is playing.

[MIX] sets the output dry/wet levels for the input and loop signals
- 0 for only input
- 50 for matched input and loop levels
- 100 for only loops

[RESET MODE] controls when loops are wiped:
- 0 wipe when ALO is turned off, bpm tempo changes, when `bars` changes
- 1 same as 0, and wipe when all loops are off
- 2 same as 0, and wipe when a button is double-pressed within one second
- 3 same as 2, but only the double-pressed loop is wiped

Loop6 behaves differently - it outputs the loop while replacing it with the input signal for next time. So if the output is looped back to the input, it works as an overdub. If the loopback goes via an effect, then the effect will be applied each time the loop passes through.

plugins.name: 3 Band EQ
plugins.label: 3 Band EQ
plugins.comment: 3 Band Equalizer, stereo version.

plugins.name: Kars
plugins.label: Kars
plugins.comment: Simple karplus-strong plucked string synth.

plugins.name: Nekobi
plugins.label: Nekobi
plugins.comment:
The Nekobi generator is based on the legendary Roland TB-303© (*).
This polemic bass-synthesizer got quite some critiques in its early days. Soon the public realised its incredible fit to trance, house and EDM music like no other device at the time. Loyal to its inspiration, the Nekobi is a monophonic synthesizer, which means only one note can be played at a time.

Features:
Modeled by FalkTX & Sean Bolton

(*) “Other product names modeled in this software are trademarks of their respective companies that do not endorse and are not associated or affiliated with MOD.
The Roland TB-303© is a trademark or trade name of another manufacturer and was used merely to identify the product whose sound was reviewed in the creation of this product. All other trademarks are the property of their respective holders.”

plugins.name: Autotune
plugins.label: Autotune
plugins.comment:
The AT1 is used to correct pitch within the vocal range and features a few different modes. The first one is the Auto mode - with this mode, the pitch will automatically be bent to the nearest true exact semitone. When selecting the MIDI mode, the pitch will be bent to the incoming midi note. The last mode is the manual mode, in which the keyboard on the pedalboard can be used.

Features:
Modeled by x42

input.name: Filter Channel
input.comment: MIDI Channel (1..16) on which the filter is active; 0: any).

input.name: Tuning
input.comment: This sets the frequency corresponding to 'A' pitch, in other words the required tuning. This will be the default 440 Hz in most cases.

input.name: Bias
input.comment: Normally the pitch estimator will select the enabled note closest to the measured pitch. The Bias control adds some preference for the current note - this allows it to go off-tune more than would be the case otherwise.

input.name: Filter
input.comment: This sets the amount of smoothing on the pitch correction while the current note does not change. If it does change the filter is bypassed and the correction jumps immediately to the new value.

input.name: Correction
input.comment: Determines how much of the estimated pitch error gets corrected. Full correction may remove expression or vibrato.

input.name: Offset
input.comment: Adds an offset in the range of +/- two semitones to the pitch correction. With the Correction control set to zero the result is a constant pitch change.

plugins.name: Level Meter
plugins.label: Level Meter
plugins.comment:
The x42 Level Meter is a numeric audio signal level meter with a 15dB/sec falloff rate.
You can use it to analyse the level of an audio signal by keeping an eye on the RMS, Peak and Hold values.
Hold stores the highest Peak level that has been measured. There is a Reset control which resets the Hold value.
It is particularly useful for inspecting your signal chain at multiple points, to understand more about what signal levels work best for particular plugins.

plugins.name: Spectrum Analyzer
plugins.label: SpectrumAnalyzer
plugins.comment:
The x42 Spectrum Analyzer is a crude spectrum analyzer plugin with a configurable response time.
You can use it to analyse a signal's frequency contents.
It is particularly useful for inspecting your signal chain at multiple points, to understand more about the effect of certain plugins on the frequency contents.

plugins.name: MIDI Step Sequencer8x8
plugins.label: Step Seq. 8x8
plugins.comment:
A simple step sequencer. It allows to trigger MIDI note events placed on a time/note grid.
The Step-duration is configurable to musical time and can optionally be modulated for a swing-time effect.
This plugin version contains 8 notes and 8 steps.

Features:
Modeled by x42

plugins.name: Instrument Tuner
plugins.label: Tuna Tuner
plugins.comment: Musical instrument tuner with strobe characteristics

plugins.name: x42 - IR Convolver Mono
plugins.label: IR Convolver
plugins.comment:
Plugin to convolve audio signals, allowing you to load so-called Reverb Impulse Responses.
This is the mono variant, with a single input and output. A stereo variant is also available.

This plugin uses background processing and is suitable to process long impulse-responses.
Configurations up to true-stereo (4 channels) are supported.

Features:
Modeled by x42

plugins.name: x42 - IR Convolver Stereo
plugins.label: IR Convolver ST
plugins.comment:
Plugin to convolve audio signals, allowing you to load so-called Reverb Impulse Responses.
This is the stereo variant, with a dual input and output. A mono variant is also available.

This plugin uses background processing and is suitable to process long impulse-responses.
Configurations up to true-stereo (4 channels) are supported.

Features:
Modeled by x42

plugins.name: Aether
plugins.label: Aether
plugins.comment: A stereo algorithmic reverb based on Cloudseed

input.name: Mix
input.comment: dry/wet ratio

input.name: Dry Level
input.comment: Level of the dry signal mixed into the output

input.name: Predelay Level
input.comment: Level of the predelayed signal mixed into the output

input.name: Early Level
input.comment: Level of the early reflections signal mixed into the output

input.name: Late Level
input.comment: Level of the late reverberations signal mixed into the output

input.name: Interpolate
input.comment: Enables or disables linear interpolation in the late diffusion block

input.name: Width
input.comment: Stereo width of the input

input.name: Predelay
input.comment: Delay between the input signal and the first reverberations

input.name: Early Low Cut Enabled
input.comment: Enables/Disables the low cut filter applied before the signal enters the reverberation unit

input.name: Early Low Cut Cutoff
input.comment: The cutoff frequency of the low cut filter applied before the signal enters the reverberation unit

input.name: Early High Cut Enabled
input.comment: Enables/Disables the high cut filter applied before the signal enters the reverberation unit

input.name: Early High Cut Cutoff
input.comment: The cutoff frequency of the high cut filter applied before the signal enters the reverberation unit

input.name: Delay Taps
input.comment: The number of delay taps in the early reflections multitap delay

input.name: Early Delay Length
input.comment: The length of the early reflections multitap delay

input.name: Early Tap Mix
input.comment: Dry/wet ratio of the multitap delay

input.name: Early Tap Decay
input.comment: The curvature of the early reflections impulse response

input.name: Early Diffusion Stages
input.comment: The number of series allpass filters in the early reflections diffusion block

input.name: Early Diffusion Delay
input.comment: The delay in the early reflections diffusion block

input.name: Early Diffusion Mod Depth
input.comment: The modulation depth of the early reflections diffusion block delay

input.name: Early Diffusion Mod Rate
input.comment: The modulation rate of the early reflections diffusion block delay

input.name: Early Diffusion Feedback
input.comment: The feedback in the early reflections diffusion block

input.name: Late Order
input.comment: Controls the signal path in the late reverberation unit

input.name: Late Delay Lines
input.comment: The number of delay lines in the late reverberation unit

input.name: Late Delay
input.comment: The delay time in the late reverberation delay lines

input.name: Late Delay Mod Depth
input.comment: The modulation depth of the line delay

input.name: Late Line Mod Rate
input.comment: The modulation rate of the line delay

input.name: Late Delay Line Feedback
input.comment: The delay line feedback

input.name: Late Diffusion Stages
input.comment: The number of series allpass filters in the late diffusion block

input.name: Late Diffusion Delay
input.comment: The delay in the late diffusion block

input.name: Late Diffusion Mod Depth
input.comment: The modulation depth of the late diffusion block delay

input.name: Late Diffusion Mod Rate
input.comment: The modulation rate of the late diffusion block delay

input.name: Late Diffusion Feedback
input.comment: The feedback in the late diffusion block

input.name: Late Low Shelf Enabled
input.comment: Enables/Disables the late reverberations low shelf filter

input.name: Late Low Shelf Cutoff
input.comment: The cutoff frequency of the late reverberations low shelf filter

input.name: Late Low Shelf Gain
input.comment: The gain of the late reverberations low shelf filter

input.name: Late high Shelf Enabled
input.comment: Enables/Disables the late reverberations high shelf filter

input.name: Late high Shelf Cutoff
input.comment: The cutoff frequency of the late reverberations high shelf filter

input.name: Late high Shelf Gain
input.comment: The gain of the late reverberations high shelf filter

input.name: Late High Cut Enabled
input.comment: Enables/Disables the late reverberations high cut filter

input.name: Late High Cut Cutoff
input.comment: The cutoff frequency of the late reverberations high cut filter

input.name: Seed Crossmix
input.comment: How much random values for the left and right channels are mixed

input.name: Tap Seed
input.comment: The seed used by the early reflections multitap delay

input.name: Early Diffusion Seed
input.comment: The seed used by the early reflections diffusion block

input.name: Delay Seed
input.comment: The seed used by the late reverberations modulated delay line

input.name: Late Diffusion Seed
input.comment: The seed used by the late reverberations diffusion block

input.name: Early Diffusion Saturation Drive
input.comment: Intensity of the saturation in the early diffusion allpass filter feedback loops

input.name: Late Diffusion Saturation Drive
input.comment: Intensity of the saturation in the early diffusion allpass filter feedback loops

plugins.name: Neural Amp Modeler
plugins.label: Neural Amp Modeler
plugins.comment:
The Neural Amp Modeler (NAM) is an open-source guitar software plugin that creates realistic simulations of analog gear, especially guitar amplifiers.
The plugin utilizes neural amp modeling technology, which employs neural networks and machine learning algorithms to replicate the behavior and characteristics of real amplifiers.

NAM loads *.nam files, enabling users to access a vast collection of meticulously crafted amp and pedal models.

You can discover a wide range of authentic tones, from vintage classics to modern designs at https://tonehunt.org/?tags=nano-mdl with their extensive library of nam models.
For MOD units search for the models with the #nano-mdl tag, as heavier models might not work.

NAM was created by Steven Ack and the LV2 version is maintained by Mike Oliphant.

plugins.name: GxCompressor
plugins.label: GxCompressor
plugins.comment:
"Compression (or more technically Dynamic range compression) is a subtle effect primarily for electric guitar where the highest and lowest points of the sound wave are "limited". This boosts the volume of softer picked notes, while capping the louder ones, giving a more even level of volume. This is frequently used in country music, where fast clean passages can sound uneven unless artificially 'squashed'." Wikipedia

*Unofficial documentation

source: http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Compression_%28electric_guitar%29

plugins.name: GxColorSoundTonebender
plugins.label: GxCsTonebender
plugins.comment:
The ToneBender is modeled after the Colorsound ToneBender© (*).

The Colorsound ToneBender is a highly sought-after four transistor fuzz based on the original schematic of the Big Muff Pi. Nonetheless, it has its own unique sound which is often brighter. With its own bank of notable users such as Jack White, Jimmy Page and Pete Townshend, this pedal became an icon of its own.

Features:
Modeled by Guitarix

(*) “Other product names modeled in this software are trademarks of their respective companies that do not endorse and are not associated or affiliated with MOD.
The Colorsound Tone Bender© is a trademark or trade name of another manufacturer and was used merely to identify the product whose sound was reviewed in the creation of this product. All other trademarks are the property of their respective holders.”

plugins.name: GxExpander
plugins.label: GxExpander
plugins.comment:
"The expander is a compressor in reverse. There are two types of expander. In some, signals above the threshold remain at unity gain whereas signals below the threshold are reduced in gain, whereas in others the signal above the threshold also has the gain increased. Therefore you can use an expander as a noise reduction unit. Set the threshold to be just below the level of the player when playing. When the player stops the signal will fall below this threshold and the signal is reduced in gain thus reducing the noise or spill."

*Unofficial documentation

source: http://www.sae.edu/reference_material/audio/pages/Compression.htm

plugins.name: GxFlanger
plugins.label: GxFlanger
plugins.comment:
"Flanging /ˈflændʒɪŋ/ is an audio effect produced by mixing two identical signals together, one signal delayed by a small and gradually changing period, usually smaller than 20 milliseconds. This produces a swept comb filter effect: peaks and notches are produced in the resulting frequency spectrum, related to each other in a linear harmonic series. Varying the time delay causes these to sweep up and down the frequency spectrum. A flanger is an effects unit that creates this effect.

Part of the output signal is usually fed back to the input (a "re-circulating delay line"), producing a resonance effect which further enhances the intensity of the peaks and troughs. The phase of the fed-back signal is sometimes inverted, producing another variation on the flanging sound." Wikipedia

*Unofficial documentation

source: http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Flanging

plugins.name: GxMultiBandCompressor
plugins.label: GxMultiBandCompressor
plugins.comment:
"Multiband (also spelled multi-band) compressors can act differently on different frequency bands. The advantage of multiband compression over full-bandwidth (full-band, or single-band) compression is that unneeded audible gain changes or "pumping" in other frequency bands is not caused by changing signal levels in a single frequency band." Wikipedia

*Unofficial documentation

source: http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dynamic_range_compression#Multiband_compression

plugins.name: GxQuack
plugins.label: GxQuack
plugins.comment: Envelope controlled wah pedal with some extra features

plugins.name: GxRedeye Vibro Chump
plugins.label: Redeye Vibro
plugins.comment:
The Vibro Chump is a guitar-amp simulator, inspired by vintage Fender amplifiers. In similar fashion to the original amplifiers from that brand and era, the Vibro Chump sports a "Vibrato" control, which does not actually pitch-shift the sound, but instead, sounds like a really warm tremolo.
Does not include cabinet simulation, best used in conjunction with the GxCabinet plugin.

Features:
Modeled by Guitarix

plugins.name: GxSlowGear
plugins.label: GxSlowGear
plugins.comment:
GxSlowGear is inspired by the classic Boss Slow Gear guitar pedal© (*), which provides you with volume swells, based on your playing dynamics.
Although originally intended for use with a guitar, the pedal was also widely used with synthesizers, electric pianos, and many other instruments.

Features:
Modeled by Guitarix
(*) “Other product names modeled in this software are trademarks of their respective companies that do not endorse and are not associated or affiliated with MOD.
Boss Slow Gear© is trademark or trade name of other manufacturer and was used merely to identify the product whose sound was reviewed in the creation of this product.
All other trademarks are the property of their respective holders.”

plugins.name: GxTremolo
plugins.label: GxTremolo
plugins.comment:
Model of a vactrol tremolo unit by "transmogrify"
** c.f. http://sourceforge.net/apps/phpbb/guitarix/viewtopic.php?f=7&t=44&p=233&hilit=transmogrifox#p233
** http://transmogrifox.webs.com/vactrol.m

*Unofficial documentation

plugins.name: GxTubeVibrato
plugins.label: GxTubeVibrato
plugins.comment:
Attempt at a true vibrato
And it works well!
Sounds very sweet with tubes wrapped

*Unofficial documentation

plugins.name: abGate
plugins.label: abGate
plugins.comment:
The abGate is a simple and straight-forward noise gate plugin. It helps to reduce noise and prevent feedback, this is especially useful when using overdrive or distortion effects.

Features:
Plugin by Antanas Bruzas

plugins.name: Fluid Guitars
plugins.label: Fluid Guitars
plugins.comment:
This plugin contains the 'Guitar' section of the Fluid GM soundfont.
The Fluid GM soundfont was constructed in part from edited/cleaned/remixed/programmed samples found in the public domain and recordings made by Frank Wen.

plugins.name: Fluid Organs
plugins.label: Fluid Organs
plugins.comment:
This plugin contains the 'Organ' section of the Fluid GM soundfont.
The Fluid GM soundfont was constructed in part from edited/cleaned/remixed/programmed samples found in the public domain and recordings made by Frank Wen.

plugins.name: Fluid SynthLeads
plugins.label: Fluid SynthLeads
plugins.comment:
Fluid Synthleads is a generator offering a variety of sample based synth sounds. Perfect for exploring and getting familiar with different synth tones.
This plugin contains the 'Synth Leads' section of the Fluid GM soundfont.

Features:
Modeled by MOD Devices.
The Fluid GM soundfont was constructed in part from edited/cleaned/remixed/programmed samples found in the public domain and recordings made by Frank Wen.

plugins.name: Audio File
plugins.label: Audio File
plugins.comment:
This is a simple audio file player plugin.

Just upload some files in the MOD file manager section (either loops, recordings or tracks) and load up the plugin.
Transport is rolling as long as the plugin is enabled and a file is selected.
You can also sync to host transport, which will play in frame-perfect sync, useful if you need a few loops playing together.

Files are streamed from disk if longer than 30 seconds in length, otherwise loaded entirely on RAM.

Even though this player plugin can loop files, it is not an audio looper.
Audio can loop back to the beginning if enabled, but it does not resample or pitch-shift to fit the host BPM.
The audio files are played back as-is, with resampling only done when needed to match the host sample rate.

plugins.name: MIDI File
plugins.label: MIDI File
plugins.comment:
This is a simple MIDI file player plugin.

Just upload some files in the MOD file manager section (specifically MIDI songs) and load up the plugin.
Transport is rolling as long as the plugin is enabled and a file is selected.
You can also sync to host transport, so the player follows host start/stop controls.
When repeat mode is enabled, the player will restart playback from the beginning after playing the last MIDI event on the file.

Note that MIDI time is converted to wall-clock time, not beats or ticks.
This means the file is played exactly as it was made (as if in "song mode").
This ignores host BPM, as the plugin is intended to play full MIDI songs and not MIDI clips.

plugins.name: C* Click - Metronome
plugins.label: Click
plugins.comment:
A sample-accurate metronome featuring a variety of click sounds, ranging from a wooden box struck with a mallet to a synthetic beep. All click sounds are synthesised once when the plugin is loaded and then played back from memory.

Features:
Modeled by CAPS

plugins.name: C* Noisegate - Attenuate noise resident in silence
plugins.label: Noisegate
plugins.comment:
This plugin aims to reduce undesirable background noise and hum in otherwise silent passages.

When the signal's instantaneous amplitude exceeds the opening threshold, the gate is opened. The time it takes until the gate is fully open can be set with the attack control. As soon as the signal's RMS power level drops below the closing threshold, the gate closes. This takes a fixed time of 20 ms; closed gate attenuation is 60 dB.

To cope with powerline hum as often present in signals from electric guitars, a notch filter can be activated by setting the mains frequency control to a non-zero value.  The filter will prevent this frequency from contributing to the signal power measurement. This allows a low closing threshold setting without mains hum keeping the gate open unduly.  The default mains setting is 50 Hz.

Features:
Modeled by CAPS

plugins.name: C* PhaserII - Mono phaser modulated by a Lorenz fractal
plugins.label: PhaserII
plugins.comment:
The Phaser II is a modern take on this classic, well-loved effect.
Thanks to its two modulation options, you will be able to customize your sound using either a traditional sine-based periodicity or the smoothened fractal oscillation.

Features:
Modeled by CAPS

plugins.name: C* Plate - Versatile plate reverb
plugins.label: Plate
plugins.comment:
One of the most breathtaking reverb sounds of them all, a plate of sheet metal hanging in a box. The Plate plugin is based around the sound of a plate reverb, with its signature “smooth” reverb tail, and was crafted especially for a soft attack and smooth ambiance.
The Plate reverb is useful for a wide variety of sounds. It tends to get along very nicely with other reverbs or even drum kits!
Available in a stereo version.

Features:
Modeled by CAPS

plugins.name: C* Wider - Stereo image Synthesis
plugins.label: Wider
plugins.comment:
Wider increases the width of a signal.
In addition to providing a basic panorama control, a perception of stereo width is created using complementary filters on the two outputs.

Features:
Modeled by CAPS
Based on the Orban 245F Stereo Synthesizer© (*)

(*) “Other product names modeled in this software are trademarks of their respective companies that do not endorse and are not associated or affiliated with MOD.
The Orban 245F Stereo Synthesizer© is a trademark or trade name of another manufacturer and was used merely to identify the product whose sound was reviewed in the creation of this product. All other trademarks are the property of their respective holders.”

plugins.name: MDA Stereo
plugins.label: Stereo
plugins.comment: Haas delay and comb filtering

plugins.name: MDA VocInput
plugins.label: VocInput
plugins.comment: This plug-in produces a voice-like signal on the right channel to be used as a carrier signal with vocoder and other vocoder plug-ins. The input signal passes through on the left channel.

input.name: Pitch
input.comment: Oscillator pitch, or pitch transpose in tracking modes

input.name: Breath
input.comment: Amount of breath noise

input.name: S Thresh
input.comment: Sensitivity for adding sibilance / fricative noise to consonants

input.name: Max Freq
input.comment: Maximum allowed pitch - mainly used to reduce pitch tracking errors

plugins.name: 2Voices
plugins.label: 2Voices
plugins.comment:
The MOD 2Voices is a pitch-shifter featuring two outputs with independent pitch-shift controls. The pitch of the input signal can be shifted anywhere between -12 semitones down, and +24 semitones up. Particularly relevant for low-CPU use cases.

Features:
Modeled by MOD Devices

plugins.name: Compressor Advanced
plugins.label: Compressor Adv
plugins.comment:
The MOD Compressor Advanced uses the same high quality algorithm as the compressor found in the MOD Dwarf's and Duox's output processing settings.

This advanced version exposes all parameters of the algorithm, and therefore allows for more control.
For a more streamlined experience, try the MOD Compressor plugin.

Features:
Modeled by VeJa Plugins
Plugin by MOD Devices

plugins.name: BandPassFilter
plugins.label: BandPassFilter
plugins.comment:
A band pass filter, "Freq" determines its Center frequency, "Q" works on the filter bandwith and "Order" is the filter's order
(or how fast frequencies above the higher cutoff frequency and below the lower cutoff frequency will decay).
Higher the order, faster the decay.

plugins.name: Capo
plugins.label: Capo
plugins.comment:
The Capo is a pitch-shifter that can raise the pitch with a maximum of +12 semitones (steps). It is more limited than the SuperCapo, but it also uses less processing power, allowing you to use the resources for other plugins!

Features:
Modeled by MOD Devices

plugins.name: CrossOver 2
plugins.label: CrossOver
plugins.comment:
The Two Way CrossOver is a simple and effective filter for splitting your signal in two different frequency ranges. 
It acts by applying a high pass filter and a low pass filter on each other outputs.
The Frequency knob defines on which frequency you want to start filtering the signal, while the Order switch indicates how intensively the cutoff frequency will decay. 

Features:
Modeled by MOD Devices

plugins.name: CrossOver 3
plugins.label: CrossOver3
plugins.comment: This plugin receives an input signal and outputs 3 filtered signals. First one is filtered with a low pass filter (LPF), the second with a band pass filter (BPF) and the third with a high pass filter (HPF), cutoff frequency used on LPF and as the lower frequency from BPF is controlled by "Freq 1" and cutoff frequency used as the higher frequency on BPF and in HPF is controlled by "Freq 2". "Order" indicates filter's orders (or how fast frequencies above (in HPF) or below (in LPF) the cutoff frequency will decay. Higher the order, faster the decay) ."Gain 1", "Gain 2" and "Gain 3" controls the gains of all outputs respectively.

plugins.name: Gain
plugins.label: Gain
plugins.comment:
Simple mono volume gain plugin without "zipping" noise when messing around with the gain parameter.
Also available in a stereo version.

Features:
Modeled by MOD Devices

plugins.name: Gain 2x2
plugins.label: Gain 2x2
plugins.comment:
A simple stereo volume gain plugin without "zipping" noise when messing around with the gain parameter.
Also available in a mono version. 

Features:
Modeled by MOD Devices

plugins.name: Harmonizer
plugins.label: Harmonizer
plugins.comment:
The Harmonizer is a (relatively) simple harmonizer plugin that allows you to set the scale to either Major, Minor, or Minor Harmonic, and then shift up a +3rd or +6th, or down a -3rd or -6th in that scale. As with nearly all harmonizers, you will have to play in the correct key (which can also be configured).

Features:
Modeled by MOD Devices

input.name: Lowest note to change the pitch

plugins.name: IR loader cabsim
plugins.label: IR loader cabsim
plugins.comment:
A cabinet simulator plugin that loads impulse response (IR) files.

In order for your personal IR files to show up in the list of available files for this plugin, please place the files in the “Speaker Cabinet IRs” folder of your device.

This plugin is specifically created for handling speaker cabinet IRs, this plugin is not optimized for handling larger files like reverb IRs.

Currently it only uses the first 42.7 ms (2048 samples at 48 kHz sampling rate) of the loaded IR file.
IR files at different sample rates are resampled to 48 kHz by the plugin.
It is recommended to trim any silence at the start of the IR file for optimal results.

Features:
Plugin by MOD Devices
Default IR file by forward audio

plugins.name: Audio to CV
plugins.label: Audio to CV
plugins.comment:
Audio to CV converts an audio signal to a control voltage signal.

Features:
Plugin by MOD Devices

plugins.name: TAP Fractal Doubler
plugins.label: Fractal Doubler
plugins.comment:
Fractal Doubler is a vocal doubler which gives you full control over your doubling.
Choose your presets for great results or tweak the controls to find your sound signature.
It also produces interesting results with acoustic/electric guitars, bass and just about any other instrument.

Features:
Modeled by TAP

plugins.name: floaty
plugins.label: floaty
plugins.comment:
Floaty is a wild modulation/delay effect that is reminiscent of a tape delay. One of the key features is the variable playback speed, allowing for repeats that ascend/descend in pitch.

There are six controls available to tame the beast: three common delay parameters (TIME, MIX and FEEDBACK) and three more special ones. WARP sets the amount of modulation with the left side being mellow and the right side being fast. TONE sets a combination of bandpass filter frequency and resonance. RATE sets the playback speed anywhere between -200% and +200%.
Disclaimer: the delay buffer is cleared whenever the TIME parameter gets a new value.

Features:
Plugin by remaincalm

plugins.name: mud
plugins.label: mud
plugins.comment:
Mud is a dark and dirty modulation/filter effect. It is particularly useful to fans of grittier, more lo-fi sounds, but it is capable of interesting univibe-style modulation too.

MIX blends between the dry and processed signal, FILTER sets a combination of bandpass filter frequency and resonance and LFO sets a combination of speed and depth for the effect, with the left side being deep and the right side being mellow.

Features:
Plugin by remaincalm

plugins.name: the infamous power cut
plugins.label: powercut
plugins.comment: This is a tape stop with up to 10 seconds of decay time. Curvature can be added for full customization of the effect.

plugins.name: Bitta
plugins.label: Bitta
plugins.comment:
OpenAV Bitta is a simple bit-crusher plugin with just two basic yet effective controls.
The Crush parameter changes the number of bits used. The Mix parameter allows you to set the blend between the dry, and the bit-crushed signal.

Features:
Modeled by OpenAV

plugins.name: Bollie Delay
plugins.label: Bollie Delay
plugins.comment:
The Bollie Delay is a stereo tempo delay featuring high pass and low pass filter as well as a host tempo function, granting more control over your delay settings. This powerful delay is perfect for experimenting with your vocals or your guitar. 

Features:
Modeled by Bollie

plugins.name: AudioToCV Pitch
plugins.label: AudioToCV Pitch
plugins.comment:
The Audio To CV Pitch plugin is a tool that turns your audio signal into CV pitch and CV gate signals.
This allows audio from instruments (such as guitars) to play and control synth sounds and effects.

It detects the pitch in your incoming audio signal and outputs a 1V/Oct CV pitch signal on the top output.
The bottom output carries the CV gate signal, it sends out 10V while a pitch is detected, and resets to 0V when the pitch can no longer be detected.

The Sensitivity parameter can be increased to detect quieter signals, or decreased to reduce artifacts.
The Octave parameter allows you to shift the detected pitch up or down by a maximum of 4 octaves. When set to 0, it will output the same pitch as is detected on the input.

The plugin also features 3 parameters that are not visible on the plugin interface:
 - The Hold Pitch parameter sets whether the plugin resets its outputs to 0, or holds the last detected pitch.
 - Increase the Confidence Threshold to make sure the correct pitch is being output, or decrease it to get a faster response time.
 - The Tolerance parameter influences how quickly you can change pitch, turn it down for a more accurate pitch output, or turn it up to make it easier to jump from one pitch to the next.

Features:
Plugin by MOD Devices & DISTRHO
Initial work by Bram Giesen

plugins.name: Record-Mono
plugins.label: Record-Mono
plugins.comment:
Mono Recorder plugin, save audio stream as wav or ogg files.

Features:
Developed by Brummer

input.name: REC
input.comment: recorded files goes to /data/user-files/Audio Recordings/lv2record/

plugins.name: Record-Quad
plugins.label: Record-Quad
plugins.comment:
Quad Reocrder plugin, save audio stream as 4 channel wav or ogg files.

Features:
Developed by Brummer

input.name: REC
input.comment: recorded files goes to /data/user-files/Audio Recordings/lv2record/

plugins.name: Record-Stereo
plugins.label: Record-Stereo
plugins.comment:
Stereo Reocrder plugin, save audio stream as wav or ogg files.

Features:
Developed by Brummer

input.name: REC
input.comment: recorded files goes to /data/user-files/Audio Recordings/lv2record/

plugins.name: ChowCentaur
plugins.label: ChowCentaur
plugins.comment:
Chow Centaur is a digital emulation of the Klon Centaur guitar pedal (*)

The plugin contains two modes:
- "Traditional" uses traditional circuit modelling techniques
- "Neural" uses a recurrent neural network to model the gain stage from the original circuit

Features:
Modeled by Jatin Chowdhury

(*) 'Product names and trademarks are the property of their respective holders that do not endorse and are not associated or affiliated with MOD Audio or Klon Centaur; they were used merely to identify the product whose sound was incorporated in the creation of this plugin.'

plugins.name: Dragonfly Hall Reverb
plugins.label: Hall Reverb
plugins.comment:
Dragonfly Hall Reverb is a high fidelity reverb effect.
It is primarily intended for use as a concert hall reverb.
It also has numerous smaller presets representing rooms, studios, and chambers. 

Please note that the Dry Level, Early Level, Early Send and Late Level controls are decoupled from the preset.
This allows you to set the levels and explore the different presets without the levels changing.

The plugin has basic controls to set the Size, Width, Predelay and Decay.
There are also more advanced controls to further tweak the reverb: Diffuse, Modulation, Spin and Wander.
Finally, there are two filter sections (High and Low) with Cut, Cross and Mult controls.

Features:
Plugin by Michael Willis
Based on Hibiki Reverb from Freeverb3 VST
GUI by FistfulOfStars

author.name: Michael Willis and Rob vd Berg

plugins.name: ADClip7
plugins.label: ADClip7
plugins.comment: The ultimate Airwindows loudness maximizer/peak control

plugins.name: Capacitor2
plugins.label: Capacitor2
plugins.comment: Capacitor with extra analog modeling and mojo

plugins.name: StarChild
plugins.label: StarChild
plugins.comment: Weird digital ambience/echo plugin.

plugins.name: Cabinet Loader
plugins.label: Cabinet Loader
plugins.comment:
The MOD Cabinet Loader enables you to play through your favorite cabinet impulse responses (IRs).

The plugin is very easy to use: Simply select the IR file, set the level as desired and off you go.

IRs can be uploaded using the file manager in either WAV and FLAC format.
Multi-channel files are supported but always processed in mono mode using the first channel.
For faster loading it is recommended to use WAV format files with 48kHz and 24 bit.

Features:
Plugin by MOD Audio & DISTRHO, based on HiFi-LoFi FFTConvolver engine.

plugins.name: Convolution Loader
plugins.label: Convolution Loader
plugins.comment:
The MOD Convolution Loader enables you to easily create custom reverb and other effects using impulse responses (IRs).

Using a very resource effective processing, it allows IRs with a length of up to 15s on the MOD Dwarf and MOD Duo X, less on the MOD Duo.

IRs can be uploaded using the file manager in either WAV and FLAC format.
Multi-channel files are supported but always processed in stereo mode.
For faster loading it is recommended to use WAV format files with 48kHz and 24 bit.

The plugin is very easy to use: Simply select the IR file, set the levels as desired, optionally filter out some low end with the built-in high pass filter, and off you go.

There is a Trails switch in the advanced settings;
when enabled the reverb tail peacefully decays when you bypass the plugin, disable it and the tail will immediately disappear when bypassed.

Features:
Plugin by MOD Audio & DISTRHO, based on HiFi-LoFi FFTConvolver engine.

plugins.name: bluesbreaker
plugins.label: bluesbreaker
plugins.comment:
The BluesBreaker a low-gain pedal with a moderate overdrive and subtle boost.

Features:
Developed by Brummer

plugins.name: DIE Fluid Synth
plugins.label: DIE Fluid Synth
plugins.comment:
The DIE Fluid Synth is a generator plugin that makes use of SF2 Instrument files (with support for the unofficial vorbis/flac-encoded SF3 format).
Upload your own instruments to your MOD Device through the file manager, and use this plugin to play them by sending MIDI notes to its input.
The entire file is loaded into RAM.

Some SF2 Instrument files contain multiple patches, and some are even multi-timbral, using a MIDI channel per timbre.
MIDI program changes can be used to change the instrument patch for the respective MIDI channel.

The plugin can tweak the chorus and reverb defined in the SF2 instrument.
The chorus is capable of a wide range of sounds, from thickening the sound to all-out wacky modulation, while the reverb helps giving that finishing touch to the sound.

Features:
Original plugin from Ardour Community Effects, based on Fluid Synth engine
Adapted & maintained by DISTRHO
GUI by MOD Devices

plugins.name: Dragonfly Early Reflections
plugins.label: Early Reflections
plugins.comment:
Dragonfly Early Reflections is a reverb effect based on Moorer's early reflection model.
This is the same algorithm used in Dragonfly Hall and Dragonfly Room, but without any late reverberation algorithm.

There are eight Reflection types to pick from; The names are primarily meant to be memorable, and are only vaguely related to how each reflection type sounds.

The plugin has controls to set the Wet Level, Dry Level, Size and Width. It also comes with post-reverb Low cut and High cut parameters.

Features:
Plugin by Michael Willis
Based on Freeverb3 VST
GUI by FistfulOfStars

plugins.name: Dragonfly Plate Reverb
plugins.label: Plate Reverb
plugins.comment:
Dragonfly Plate is an easy to use, high fidelity plate reverb plugin.

It comes with a variety of plate and tank presets for you to explore.
Please note that Wet level and Dry level controls are decoupled from the preset.
This allows you to set the levels and explore the different presets without the levels changing.

There are three different Reverb types available:
- Simple, a thin plate algorithm with a sparse tail and some slight early reflections
- Nested, a thick plate algorithm with a dense tail and no early reflections
- Tank, a tank algorithm based on Jon Dattorro's Effect Design paper

The plugin has controls to set the Width, Predelay and Decay.
It also comes with pre-reverb Low cut and High cut parameters, and a Dampen control which controls a low pass filter applied to the reverb algorithm's internal feedback loop.

Features:
Plugin by Michael Willis
Based on Nverb and STRev from Freeverb3 VST
GUI by FistfulOfStars

plugins.name: Dragonfly Room Reverb
plugins.label: Room Reverb
plugins.comment:
Dragonfly Room Reverb is a high fidelity reverb effect.
It has several different room presets, as well as a small number of hall presets and effect presets.

Please note that the Dry Level, Early Level, Early Send and Late Level controls are decoupled from the preset.
This allows you to set the levels and explore the different presets without the levels changing.

The plugin has basic controls to set the Size, Width, Predelay and Decay.
There are also more advanced controls to further tweak the reverb: Diffuse, Spin and Wander.
Finally, there are two filter sections with controls for: High Cut, Early Damp, Late Damp, Low Cut, Low Boost and Boost Freq.

Features:
Plugin by Michael Willis
Based on ProG Reverb from Freeverb3VST
GUI by FistfulOfStars
